title: Time Shifting
summary: This page presents Example 1.3 on time shifting of signals, demonstrating how an exponential function x(t) = e^(-2t) is delayed and advanced by 1 second. The mathematical description of the original signal is provided, and figures illustrate the original signal alongside its right-shifted (delayed) and left-shifted (advanced) versions. The delayed function is represented as x(t-1), corresponding to a right shift of the signal.

figures.caption: Signal x(t) = e^{-2t} u(t) shown in (a), delayed by 1 second in (b), and advanced by 1 second in (c).
